blocks:
سُورة لقمان

مكّيّة وهي 34 آية

(١) الم (1) Alif, Lām, Mīm. (٢) تِلْكَ آياتُ الْكِتابِ الْحَكِيمِ ** (2) These are the verses of the wise Book, Possessing wisdom (ذي الحكمة) or whose verses are made firm (المحكم آياته). (٣) هُدىً وَرَحْمَةً ** (3) a guidance and a mercy And it was recited with the rafʿ (بالرَّفع) [as hudan and raḥmatun], for the virtuous, (٤) الَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ الصَّلاةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ الزَّكاةَ وَهُمْ بِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ ** (4) those who maintain the prayer and pay the zakat, and are certain of the Hereafter. An explanation of their virtue (إحسانهم), or a specification of these three [acts] from its branches (شعبه) due to the great importance attached to them. (٥) أُولئِكَ عَلى هُدىً مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ ** (5) It is they who are guided by their Lord, and it is they who are the felicitous. Because they have gathered together the true creed (العقيدة الحقّة) and righteous action (العمل الصالح). (٦) وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يَشْتَرِي لَهْوَ الْحَدِيثِ ** (6) And among the people is he who buys diversionary talk That which distracts from what is important (عمّا يعني), such as narratives (الاحاديث) that have no basis (لا أصل لها), legends (الأساطير) that hold no significance (لا اعتبار فيها), jokes (المضاحيك), and superfluous speech (فضول الكلام). Al-Qummi said: [It refers to] singing (الغناء), drinking wine (شرب الخمر), and all forms of amusement (جميع الملاهي). The full discussion concerning this will come shortly. لِيُضِلَ ** that he may lead astray And it was recited with the fatḥah on the yāʾ [as liyaḍilla]. عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** from the way of Allah Al-Qummi said: He turns them away (يحيدهم) from His path. بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ** without any knowledge, Regarding the state of what he buys. وَيَتَّخِذَها ** and takes it And it was recited with the naṣb (بالنّصب) [as wa yattakhidhahā]. هُزُواً ** in derision. And he takes the way (السبيل) as mockery (سخريّة). For such there is a humiliating punishment, because of their humiliation of the truth (لاهانتهم الحقّ) by preferring falsehood (الباطل) over it.

(٧) وَإِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِ آياتُنا وَلَّى مُسْتَكْبِراً ** (7) And when Our signs are recited to him, he turns his back in arrogance, being arrogant, not caring about them ** كَأَنْ لَمْ يَسْمَعْها كَأَنَّ فِي أُذُنَيْهِ وَقْراً ** as if he had not heard them, as if there were a deafness in his ears. a heaviness (ثقلاً) such that he is unable to hear ** فَبَشِّرْهُ بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ ** So inform him of a painful punishment. inform him of it, and the mention of "good news" (البشارة) is only used sarcastically (على التهكّم).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He is al-Nadr ibn al-Harith ibn 'Alqamah ibn Kaldah from the tribe of Banu 'Abd al-Dar ibn Qusayy. Al-Nadr was known for narrating the traditions (أحاديث) and poems (أشعارهم) of the people. Allah the Almighty says: وَإِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِ آياتُنا (And when Our signs are recited to him) [up to] the end of the Ayah.

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: It is the slandering of the truth (الطعن في الحق) and mocking it, and what Abu Jahl and his companions used to bring forth when he said: "O assembly of Quraysh, shall I not feed you some of the Zaqqum that your companion frightens you with?" Then he sent for butter and dates and said: "This is the Zaqqum that he frightens you with." He (peace be upon him) said: And among it (منه) is singing (الغناء).

And in al-Ma'ani and al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), He said: Among it (منه) is singing (الغناء). And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Singing (الغناء) is among that for which Allah has promised the Fire, and He recited this Ayah.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the earnings (كسب) of female singers (المغنّيات). He said: "That one whom men enter upon is unlawful (حرام), but the one who is invited to weddings (الأعراس) has no harm in it (ليس به بأس)." And this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: ومن النّاس (And among the people) [up to] the end of the Ayah.

(٨) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتُ النَّعِيمِ ** (8) Indeed, those who have faith and do righteous deeds—there will be gardens of bliss for them, Meaning: For them is the bliss (نعيم) of gardens (جنّات), but it was inverted (عكس) for the sake of emphasis (للمبالغة). (٩) خالِدِينَ فِيها وَعْدَ اللهِ حَقًّا وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ ** (9) to abide in them forever—Allah’s promise, which is true. He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. Who is not overcome by anything, preventing Him from fulfilling His promise and His threat, الْحَكِيمُ ** Who does not do anything except what His wisdom (حكمته) necessitates. (١٠) خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ بِغَيْرِ عَمَدٍ تَرَوْنَها ** (10) He created the heavens without any pillars that you can see, A description (صفة) for pillars (عمد). In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Then عَمَدٍ ** (pillars), but you do not see them. وَأَلْقى فِي الْأَرْضِ رَواسِيَ ** (and cast firm mountains in the earth) meaning: towering mountains (جبالاً شوامخ) أَنْ تَمِيدَ بِكُمْ ** (lest it should shake with you) meaning: lest it should incline (تميل) with you. It was said (قيل) that the simplicity (بساطة) of its parts (اجزائها) necessitates the change (تبدّل) of their locations (أحيازها) and positions (أوضاعها), due to the impossibility of each part being specified, either by itself or by something necessary to it (لوازمه), to a specific location (حيّز) and position (وضع معينين). وَبَثَّ فِيها مِنْ كُلِّ دابَّةٍ وَأَنْزَلْنا مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَأَنْبَتْنا فِيها مِنْ كُلِّ زَوْجٍ كَرِيمٍ (and He scattered in it every kind of animal. And We sent down water from the sky, and caused to grow therein every noble pair.) Of every kind (صنف) that is abundant in benefit (كثير المنفعة).

(١١) هذا خَلْقُ اللهِ His creation (مخلوقه) فَأَرُونِي ما ذا خَلَقَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ دُونِهِ so that they deserved partnership with Him in divinity (الألوهيّة) بَلِ الظَّالِمُونَ فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ This is a shift from reproaching them to confirming error (الضلال) against them.

(11) This is Allah’s creation. So show Me what those besides Him have created. Rather the wrongdoers are in manifest error.

(12) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا لُقْمانَ الْحِكْمَةَ. (12) Certainly We gave Luqmān wisdom) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), he said: "Understanding (الفهم) and intellect (العقل)." And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "He was granted the knowledge (معرفة) of the Imam of his time." أَنِ اشْكُرْ لِلَّهِ وَمَنْ يَشْكُرْ فَإِنَّما يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِ ** (—[saying]: 'Be grateful to Allah. And whoever is grateful, is grateful only for his own sake.) because its benefit returns to it (the soul), and that is the permanence of the blessing (النعمة) and the deserving of its increase. وَمَنْ كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَنِيٌ ** (And whoever is ungrateful, [let him know that] Allah is indeed Self-sufficient,) He has no need for gratitude, حَمِيدٌ ** (All-laudable.) worthy of praise (الحمد), whether He is praised or not praised, or He is Praised (محمود) Whom all His creatures articulate His praise.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The gratitude (شكر) for every blessing (نعمة), however great it may be, is that one praises Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, for it." And in a narration: "And if there is a right (حقّ) concerning what He has bestowed upon him, he fulfills it." And in another narration from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever is blessed by Allah with a blessing (نعمة) and recognizes it with his heart, he has fulfilled its gratitude (شكرها)." And from him (peace be upon him): "Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to Moses (peace be upon him): 'O Moses, thank Me with the gratitude that is due to Me (حقّ شكري).' So he said: 'O Lord, how can I thank You with the gratitude that is due to You, when there is no gratitude (شكر) with which I thank You except that You have bestowed it upon me?' He said: 'O Moses, now you have thanked Me, when you knew that this is from Me.'"

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, he said: "Truly I say, Luqman was not a prophet (نبيّاً), but he was a servant (عبداً) who engaged much in contemplation (التفكّر), possessed excellent certainty (اليقين). He loved Allah, so He loved him and bestowed upon him the wisdom (الحكمة). He was sleeping at midday when a call came to him: 'O Luqman, would you like Allah to make you a Caliph (خليفة) on earth, judging between people with justice (بالحقّ)?' He answered the voice: 'If my Lord gives me a choice, I would accept well-being (العافية) and not accept tribulation (البلا). But if He resolves it upon me, then [I offer] hearing and obedience (سمعاً وطاعة), for I know that if He does that to me, He will assist me and protect me.' The angels, in a voice he could hear but not see them, said: 'Why, O Luqman?' He said: 'Because judgment (الحكم) is the most difficult and most confirmed of stations (المنازل). Injustice (الظُّلم) covers it from every direction. If he fulfills [his duty], he is likely to be saved, but if he errs, he errs from the path of Paradise. And for one to be humble (ذليلاً) in this world and noble (شريفاً) in the Hereafter is better than for one to be noble in this world and humble in the Hereafter. And whoever chooses this world over the Hereafter, this world will escape him, and he will not attain the Hereafter.' The angels were astonished by the excellence of his speech. Then he slept a short sleep and was granted the wisdom (الحكمة). He awoke speaking with it. Then he used to assist David (peace be upon him) with his wisdom. David said to him: 'Blessed are you, O Luqman! You were given the wisdom and tribulation (البلوى) was averted from you.'"

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about Luqman and the wisdom (حكمته) which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, mentioned. He said: "By Allah, Luqman was not granted the wisdom (الحكمة) due to lineage (حسب), nor wealth (مال), nor family (أهل), nor physical stature (بسط في جسم), nor beauty (جمال). Rather, he was a man strong in the command of Allah, extremely pious (متورّعاً) regarding Allah, silent (ساكتاً), very quiet (سكّيتاً), deep in contemplation (عميق النظر), long in thought (طويل الفكر), sharp in insight (حديد النظر), and content with lessons (العبر). He never slept during the day, never reclined in a gathering, never spat in a gathering, never trifled with anything, and no one ever saw him while urinating, defecating, or bathing, due to the intensity of his concealment (تستّره), the depth of his insight (عموق نظره), and his carefulness (تحفّظه) in his affairs. He never laughed at anything, fearing sin (الإثم) in his religion. He never became angry, and he never joked with anyone. He never rejoiced over anything he was given of this world if it came to him, nor did he ever grieve over anything of it. He married women and had many children, and most of them died young (أفراطاً), yet he never wept over the death of any of them. He never passed by two men disputing or fighting except that he reconciled between them, and he would not leave them until they loved one another. He never heard a saying from anyone that he found good except that he asked him about its explanation (تفسيره) and from whom he took it. He used to frequent the company of jurists (الفقهاء) and wise men (الحكماء). He used to visit judges (القضاة), kings (الملوك), and rulers (السلاطين), and he would feel pity for the judges because of the trials (ابتلوا به) they were afflicted with, and he would have mercy on the kings and rulers due to their pride in Allah and their complacency in that. He would take heed (يعتبر) and learn what he could use to overcome his soul (نفسه), strive against his desires (هواه), and guard himself against Satan. He used to treat his heart with contemplation (التفكّر) and treat his soul with lessons (العبر). He would only travel (يظعن) for what benefited him, and he would only look at what concerned him. It was by this that he was granted the wisdom (الحكمة) and bestowed with infallibility (العصمة)."

"And Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, commanded groups of angels when midday arrived and eyes rested for the siesta (القائلة). They called out to Luqman, where he could hear them but not see them, and they said: 'O Luqman, would you like Allah to make you a Caliph (خليفة) on earth, judging between people?' Luqman said: 'If my Lord commands me to do that, then [I offer] hearing and obedience (السمع والطاعة), because if He does that to me, He will assist me in it, teach me, and protect me. But if He gives me a choice, I would accept well-being (العافية).'"

The angels said: "O Luqman, why did you say that?" He said: "Because judging between people is one of the most difficult stations (المنازل) of the religion, and the most full of trials (فتناً) and tribulations (بلاء). He is neither abandoned nor assisted, and injustice (الظُّلم) covers him from every direction. Its practitioner is between two matters: if he hits the truth (الحقّ) in it, he is likely to be safe; but if he errs, he errs from the path of Paradise. And for one to be humble (ذليلاً) and weak (ضعيفاً) in this world is easier for him in the Return (المعاد) than for him to be a noble (سريا) and honorable (شريفاً) judge in it. And whoever chooses this world over the Hereafter loses both of them; this one vanishes, and he does not attain that one." He said: "So the angels were astonished by his wisdom, and the All-beneficent (الرّحمن) approved of his speech. When evening came and he took his resting place at night, Allah sent down the wisdom (الحكمة) upon him, covering him with it from the crown of his head to his foot while he was sleeping, and He covered him with wisdom as a covering. He awoke, and he was the wisest of people in his time, and he went out among the people speaking with wisdom and spreading it among them."

He said: "When he was offered the authority (الحكم) of the Caliphate (الخلافة) and did not accept it, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded the angels to call David (peace be upon him) to the Caliphate, and he accepted it without stipulating the condition Luqman had stipulated. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, granted him the Caliphate on earth, and he was tested in it more than once, and in all of that he fell into error, which Allah the Almighty accepted and forgave him for. Luqman used to frequently visit David (peace be upon him) and advise him with his admonitions (مواعظه), his wisdom (حكمته), and the excellence of his knowledge (علمه). David (peace be upon him) used to say to him: 'Blessed are you, O Luqman! You were granted the wisdom, and tribulation (البليّة) was averted from you. And David was given the Caliphate and was tested by judgment (الحكم) and trial (الفتنة).'"

(١٣) ** وَإِذْ قالَ لُقْمانُ لِابْنِهِ وَهُوَ يَعِظُهُ يا بُنَيَ ** (13) And when Luqmān said to his son, advising him: 'O my son!' (It is) a diminutive form indicating compassion (اشفاق). And it was recited with the kasrah on the yā' and with its sukūn. **لا تُشْرِكْ بِاللهِ إِنَّ الشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌ ** (13) 'Do not ascribe any partners to Allah. Indeed, polytheism is a great injustice.' because it is an equating between the One from Whom there is no blessing except from Him, and the one from whom there is no blessing.

In al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Injustice (الظلم) is of three types: injustice that Allah forgives, injustice that Allah does not forgive, and injustice that Allah does not leave (unsettled). As for the injustice that Allah does not forgive, it is polytheism (الشرك). And as for the injustice that He forgives, it is a man's injustice to himself in what is between him and Allah. And as for the injustice that Allah does not leave (unsettled), it is the mutual dealings (المداينة) between the servants.

(١٤) وَوَصَّيْنَا الْإِنْسانَ بِوالِدَيْهِ حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُ وَهْناً عَلى وَهْنٍ ** (14) We have enjoined man concerning his parents: his mother carried him through weakness upon weakness, She weakens, a weakness upon weakness, for her weakness continues to multiply. And it was read with the fath on the hā (وَهْناً). وَفِصالُهُ فِي عامَيْنِ ** (and his weaning is in two years.) And his weaning (فطام) is at the expiration of two years, and she used to suckle him during that period. And the two sentences are parenthetical clauses (اعتراض) confirming the injunction (التوصية) regarding her right. أَنِ اشْكُرْ لِي وَلِوالِدَيْكَ إِلَيَّ الْمَصِيرُ ** (We told him:) 'Give thanks to Me and to your parents. The return is to Me. So I shall hold you accountable for your gratitude and your ingratitude. In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "And He commanded us with gratitude to Him and to the parents, so whoever does not thank his parents has not thanked Allah." And from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever does not thank the benefactor (المنعم) among the creatures has not thanked Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ)."

(١٥) وَإِنْ جاهَداكَ عَلى أَنْ تُشْرِكَ بِي ما لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ ** (15) But if they urge you to ascribe partners to Me, of which you have no knowledge, regarding its deserving partnership, merely following them. This means: فَلا تُطِعْهُما ** then do not obey them in that matter. وَصاحِبْهُما فِي الدُّنْيا مَعْرُوفاً ** and accompany them in this world with kindness. a well-known companionship (صحاباً معروفاً) that the Divine Law (الشرع) approves and generosity (الكرم) necessitates.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): A man came to the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny) and said: "O Messenger of Allah, advise me." He said: "Do not associate anything with Allah, even if you are burned by fire and tortured, unless your heart is content with faith. And [regarding] your parents, obey them and treat them kindly (برّهما), whether they are alive or dead. And if they command you to leave your family (أهلك) and your wealth (مالك), then do so, for that is part of faith."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): A man came to the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny) and said: "O Messenger of Allah, whom should I treat kindly (أبرّ)?" He said: "Your mother." He said: "Then whom?" He said: "Your mother." He said: "Then whom?" He said: "Your mother." He said: "Then whom?" He said: "Your father."

And from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked: "Should I supplicate for my parents if they do not recognize the Truth (الحق)?" He said: "Supplicate for them and give charity on their behalf. And if they are alive and do not recognize the Truth (الحق), then deal gently with them (فدارهما), for the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) said: 'Indeed, Allah sent me with mercy (الرّحمة), not with disobedience (العقوق).'"

And in al-Uyun, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Kindness to parents (برّ الوالدين) is obligatory, even if they are polytheists (مشركين). However, there is no obedience to them in disobedience to the Creator (معصية الخالق), nor to anyone else, for there is no obedience to a creature in disobedience to the Creator (معصية الخالق). And in Misbah al-Shari'ah, Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: Kindness to parents (برّ الوالدين) is part of the servant's excellent knowledge (حسن معرفة) of Allah, for no act of worship (عبادة) reaches its doer to the pleasure of Allah, the Exalted, faster than honoring Muslim parents (حرمة الوالدين المسلمين) for the sake of Allah, the Exalted. This is because the right of the parents (حقّ الوالدين) is derived from the right of Allah, provided they are upon the path of the religion (منهاج الدّين) and the Sunnah, and they do not prevent the child from obedience to Allah toward disobedience, nor from certainty (اليقين) toward doubt (الشكّ), nor from asceticism (الزّهد) toward the world (الدنيا), nor do they call him to the opposite of that. If they are like that, then disobedience to them is obedience [to Allah], and obedience to them is disobedience [to Allah]. Allah, the Exalted, said: وَإِنْ جاهَداكَ عَلى أَنْ تُشْرِكَ بِي ما لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ فَلا تُطِعْهُما ** (But if they urge you to ascribe partners to Me, of which you have no knowledge, then do not obey them). As for the matter of companionship (باب العشرة), deal gently with them (فدارهما), be kind to them (ارفق بهما), and bear their harm (أذاهما) just as they bore [the burden] from you during your childhood. Do not restrict them regarding what Allah has made abundant for you of food (المأكول) and clothing (الملبوس). Do not turn your face away from them, and do not raise your voice above their voices, for honoring them (تعظيمهما) is from Allah, the Exalted. And speak to them with the best and kindest speech (بأحسن القول والطفة), for Allah does not waste the reward of the virtuous (المحسنين). وَاتَّبِعْ سَبِيلَ مَنْ أَنابَ إِلَيَّ ** and follow the way of him who turns to Me. through affirming the Oneness of God (التَّوحيد), sincerity (الإخلاص), and obedience (الطاعة).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: اتَّبِعْ سَبِيلَ ** (follow the way of) Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his progeny). ثُمَّ إِلَيَّ مَرْجِعُكُمْ ** Then to Me will be your return all of you. فَأُنَبِّئْكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** whereat I will inform you concerning what you used to do. The two verses (الآيتان) are parenthetical (معترضان) within the folds of Luqman's advice (وصيّة لقمان), serving as an emphasis on the prohibition of polytheism (النهي عن الشرك) contained therein. It is as if He said: "We have advised [you] with the like of what he advised." The mention of the parents is for the sake of emphasis (للمبالغة) in this matter, for even though they follow the Creator (الباري) in deserving reverence (التعظيم) and obedience (الطاعة), it is not permissible for them to deserve [obedience] in polytheism (الإشراك). So, what do you think of others besides them?

(١٦) ** يا بُنَيَ ** And it was read with the kasrah of the yā’ (يا بُنَيِ). إِنَّها ** (1) إِنْ تَكُ مِثْقالَ حَبَّةٍ مِنْ خَرْدَلٍ ** Meaning, the characteristic (الخصلة) of wrongdoing (الإساءة) or good-doing (الإحسان) being, for example, in smallness like a mustard seed. And it was read mithqāl (مِثْقَالٌ) with the rafʿ (الرَّفع), so the hā’ (الهاء) refers to the story (القصّة) and the verb of existence (الكون) is complete (تامّة). فَتَكُنْ فِي صَخْرَةٍ أَوْ فِي السَّماواتِ أَوْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** In the most hidden place (أخفى مكان), the most protected (أحرزه), the highest (أعلاه), and the lowest (أسفله). يَأْتِ بِهَا اللهُ ** He will bring it forth and hold accountability for it. And al-Qummi said: [If it is] from the provision (الرّزق), Allah will bring it to you. إِنَّ اللهَ لَطِيفٌ ** His knowledge reaches every hidden thing (خفيّ). خَبِيرٌ ** Knowing its essence (كنهه). ** يا بُنَيَ إِنَّها إِنْ تَكُ مِثْقالَ حَبَّةٍ مِنْ خَرْدَلٍ فَتَكُنْ فِي صَخْرَةٍ أَوْ فِي السَّماواتِ أَوْ فِي الْأَرْضِ يَأْتِ بِهَا اللهُ إِنَّ اللهَ لَطِيفٌ خَبِيرٌ ** (16) O my son! Should it be the weight of a mustard seed, and lie in a rock, or in the heavens, or in the earth, Allah will bring it forth. Indeed Allah is all-subtle, all-aware.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Beware of the trivial sins (المحقّرات من الذّنوب), for they have a claimant (طالباً). Let none of you say, 'I commit a sin and then seek Allah's forgiveness,' for Allah says: إِنْ تَكُ مِثْقالَ حَبَّةٍ مِنْ خَرْدَلٍ ** [until the end of] the Ayah." He (Fayd Kashani) narrated it in al-Majma' from him (al-Sadiq) (peace be upon him), and in al-Kafi from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): the like of it.

(١٧) يا بُنَيَ ** (17) O my son! And it was read with the kasrah of the yā’ and its sukūn (إسكانها): أَقِمِ الصَّلاةَ وَأْمُرْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَانْهَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَاصْبِرْ عَلى ما أَصابَكَ ** (O my son! Maintain the prayer and enjoin the right and forbid the wrong, and be patient over what befalls you.) from the hardships (الشدائد). In al-Majma‘, narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): [It means] from the difficulty (المشقّة) and harm (الأذى) in enjoining the right (الامر بالمعروف) and forbidding the wrong (النهي عن المنكر). ** إِنَّ ذلِكَ مِنْ عَزْمِ الْأُمُورِ ** (Indeed that is among the matters resolved upon.) Its cutting (قطعه) is a cutting of obligation (إيجاب) and necessity (الزام). And from this is the hadith: "Indeed, Allah loves that His dispensations (رخصه) be taken, just as He loves that His firm obligations (عزائمه) be taken."

footnote (١) Its meaning is that the deed (*فعلة*) of man, whether good or evil, if it is the measure of a mustard seed in weight.

(١٨) وَلا تُصَعِّرْ خَدَّكَ لِلنَّاسِ ** (18) And do not turn your cheek away from the people And do not turn your face away from the people out of arrogance, nor turn away from one who speaks to you out of contempt for him. Thus is it stated in al-Majma' from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). It is said that it derives from الصَعَّر (al-sa'ar), which is a disease that afflicts the camel, causing it to twist its neck. And al-Qummi (said): Meaning, do not humble yourself to the people out of greed for what they possess. And it was recited (لا تصاعر). وَلا تَمْشِ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَحاً ** and do not walk exultantly on the earth. (Meaning) joy (فرحاً), which is insolence (البطر). And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says (meaning, do not walk) with grandeur (بالعظمة). إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتالٍ فَخُورٍ ** Indeed Allah does not like any swaggering braggart. (This is) the reason for the prohibition. In al-Majalis and al-Faqih, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He forbade a man from swaggering in his walk, and He said: "Whoever wears a garment and swaggers in it, Allah will cause him to sink from the edge (شفير) of Hell, and he will be the companion (قرين) of Qarun, because he was the first one who swaggered, and the earth swallowed him and his dwelling. And whoever swaggers has contended with Allah in His Majesty (جبروت)."

(١٩) وَاقْصِدْ فِي مَشْيِكَ ** (19) And be moderate in your gait Be moderate in it, between creeping (الدّبيب) and rushing (الإسراع). And Al-Qummi (said): meaning, do not hasten. And in al-Khisal, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The speed of walking removes the splendor (بهاء) of the believer. ** وَاغْضُضْ مِنْ صَوْتِكَ ** and lower your voice. Shorten it (lower it). And Al-Qummi (said): meaning, do not raise it. ** إِنَّ أَنْكَرَ الْأَصْواتِ ** Indeed the most repulsive of voices the harshest of them. ** لَصَوْتُ الْحَمِيرِ. ** is the donkey’s voice. In al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He (peace be upon him) was asked about it, and He said: The ugly sneeze (العطسة القبيحة). And in al-Majma', narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: It is the loud, ugly sneeze (العطسة المرتفعة القبيحة), and the man who raises his voice in speech with an ugly raising, unless he is a caller (داعياً) or reciting the Quran. And Al-Qummi narrated from Him (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah the Almighty: ** وَإِذْ قالَ لُقْمانُ لِابْنِهِ ** And when Luqman said to his son (the verses). He said: So Luqman admonished his son with such effects (باثار) that he split open (تفطّر) and cracked (وانشقّ). And among what he admonished him with was that he said: O my son, since you fell into the world, you have turned your back on it and faced the Hereafter. Thus, the abode toward which you are traveling is closer to you than the abode from which you are moving away. O my son, sit with the scholars (العُلماء) and press against them with your knees, and do not argue with them lest they prevent you (from learning). Take from the world only what suffices (بلاغا), and do not reject it entirely, lest you become a burden (عيالاً) upon people. And do not enter into it with an entrance that harms your Hereafter. And fast a fast that cuts off your desire (شهوتك), but do not fast a fast that prevents you from prayer (الصلاة), for prayer is more beloved to Allah than fasting (الصيام). O my son, indeed the world is a deep sea in which many people (عالم كثير) have perished. So make your ship in it Faith (الإيمان), make its sail Reliance (on God) (التوكّل), and make your provision in it the fear of Allah (تقوى الله). If you are saved, it is by the mercy of Allah, and if you perish, it is due to your sins. O my son, if you are disciplined (تأدّبت) when young, you will benefit from it when old. Whoever cares about discipline (الأدب) becomes concerned with it, and whoever is concerned with it strives for its knowledge, and whoever strives for its knowledge intensifies his seeking of it, and whoever intensifies his seeking of it attains its benefit. So take it as a habit, for you will succeed your predecessors (سلفك) and benefit those who succeed you (خلفك). A seeker (راغب) will hope in you regarding it, and a fearful one (راهب) will fear your assault (صولتك). Beware of laziness concerning it and seeking something other than it. If you are overcome regarding the world, do not be overcome regarding the Hereafter. If you miss seeking knowledge in its proper places (مظانّه), then you have been overcome regarding the Hereafter. And set aside for yourself a portion of your days, nights, and hours for seeking knowledge, for you will not find a greater waste of it than abandoning it. And do not argue with a stubborn person (لجوجاً) concerning it, nor dispute with a jurist (فقيهاً), nor antagonize a ruler (سلطاناً), nor walk with an oppressor (ظلوماً), nor befriend him, nor take as a brother a filthy transgressor (فاسقاً نطفاً), nor accompany one who is accused (متّهماً). And store your knowledge as you store your silver (ورقك). O my son, fear Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, with a fear such that if you came on the Day of Resurrection with the righteousness (برّ) of the two weighty ones (الثّقلين), you would fear that He might punish you. And hope in Allah with a hope such that if you met the Resurrection with the sin (إثم) of the two weighty ones, you would hope that Allah would forgive you. His son said to him: O my father, how can I endure this when I have only one heart? Luqman said to him: O my son, if the heart of the believer were extracted and split open, two lights would be found in it: a light for fear and a light for hope. If they were weighed, neither would outweigh the other by the weight of a speck (مثقال ذرّة). So whoever believes in Allah confirms what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has said. And whoever confirms what Allah has said performs what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has commanded. And whoever does not perform what Allah has commanded has not confirmed what Allah has said. For these characteristics (الأخلاق) bear witness to one another. So whoever believes in Allah with sincere faith (ايماناً صادقاً), working for Allah sincerely (خالصاً) and advising (ناصحاً), has truly believed in Allah sincerely. And whoever obeys Allah fears Him, and whoever fears Him has loved Him, and whoever loves Him has followed His command, and whoever follows His command deserves His Paradise and His pleasure (مرضاته). And whoever does not follow the pleasure (رضوان) of Allah, the wrath (سخط) of Allah has become insignificant to him. We seek refuge in Allah from the wrath of Allah. O my son, do not incline toward the world (لا تركن إلى الدنيا) and do not occupy your heart with it. For Allah has not created any creation that is more insignificant (اهون) to Him than it. Do you not see that He did not make its comfort (نعيمها) a reward for the obedient, nor did He make its affliction (بلاؤها) a punishment for the disobedient?

(٢٠) أَلَمْ تَرَوْا أَنَّ اللهَ سَخَّرَ لَكُمْ ما فِي السَّماواتِ ** (20) Have you not regarded that Allah has subjected to you whatever is in the heavens by making it causes (أسباباً) for your benefits, وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ and whatever is in the earth by enabling you to benefit from it, وَأَسْبَغَ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعَمَهُ ظاهِرَةً وَباطِنَةً and He has showered upon you His blessings, manifest and hidden—sensible (محسوسة) and intelligible (معقولة), what you know and what you do not know. And نعمه (His blessings) was read in the plural form.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: "As for the manifest blessing (النعمة الظاهرة), it is the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and what He brought concerning the knowledge of Allah and His Oneness (توحيده). And as for the hidden blessing (النعمة الباطنة), it is our wilayah (ولايتنا), the People of the House (أهل البيت), and the covenant of our affection (مودّتنا)."

In al-Ikmal and al-Manaqib, narrated from al-Kazim عليه السلام: "The manifest blessing (النّعمة الظاهرة) is the manifest Imam (الامام الظاهر), and the hidden blessing (الباطنة) is the absent Imam (الامام الغائب)."

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "As for what is manifest (ما ظهر), it is Islam, and what Allah has perfected of your creation, and what He has bestowed upon you of provision (الرزق). And as for what is hidden (ما بطن), it is His concealment of the flaws of your deeds, and His not exposing you thereby."

In al-Amali, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: "The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to Ali عليه السلام: 'Say: What is the first blessing (نعمة) with which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, tested you and bestowed upon you?' He said: 'That He, exalted be His praise, created me when I was not a thing mentioned.' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully. So what is the second?' He said: 'That He was good to me when He created me, and made me living, not inanimate (مواتاً).' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully. So what is the third?' He said: 'That He originated me—and all praise belongs to Him—in the best form and the most balanced structure (تركيب).' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully. So what is the fourth?' He said: 'That He made me one who reflects (متفكرّاً), watchful (راعياً), not heedless (ساهياً).' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully. So what is the fifth?' He said: 'That He made for me senses (شواعر) by which I perceive what I seek, and He made for me an illuminating lamp (سراجاً منيراً).' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully. So what is the sixth?' He said: 'That Allah guided me to His religion and did not lead me astray from His path (سبيله).' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully. So what is the seventh?' He said: 'That He made for me a return (مردّا) to a life that has no cessation.' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully. So what is the eighth?' He said: 'That He made me a king (ملكاً), an owner (مالكاً), not owned (مملوكاً).' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully. So what is the ninth?' He said: 'That He subjected to me His heaven and His earth, and what is in them, and what is between them of His creation.' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully. So what is the tenth?' He said: 'That He, glory be to Him, made us males (ذكراناً), maintainers (قواماً) over our wives (حلائلنا), not females (اناثاً).' He said: 'You have spoken truthfully. So what is after that?' He said: 'The blessings of Allah have multiplied, O Prophet of Allah, and they are good.'"

وَإِنْ تَعُدُّوا نِعْمَةَ اللهِ لا تُحْصُوها ** and should you enumerate Allah’s blessings, you cannot count them. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم smiled and said: "May wisdom be pleasing to you! May knowledge be pleasing to you, O Abu al-Hasan! For you are the inheritor of my knowledge and the clarifier (المبيّن) for my Ummah concerning what they differ about after me." (The Hadith).

وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يُجادِلُ فِي اللهِ ** Yet among the people are those who dispute concerning Allah regarding His Oneness (توحيده) and His attributes (صفاته), بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ without any knowledge derived from proof (برهان), وَلا هُدىً or any guidance referring back to a Messenger or a successor (وصيّ) of a Messenger, وَلا كِتابٍ مُنِيرٍ or any illuminating Book that Allah has sent down, but rather by imitating (تقليد) those whose imitation is not permissible.

(٢١) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمُ اتَّبِعُوا ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ قالُوا بَلْ نَتَّبِعُ ما وَجَدْنا عَلَيْهِ آباءَنا أَوَلَوْ كانَ الشَّيْطانُ يَدْعُوهُمْ إِلى عَذابِ السَّعِيرِ (21) And when they are told, 'Follow what Allah has sent down,' they say, 'No, but we follow what we found our fathers following.' What, even if Satan invites them to the punishment of the Blaze?

Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It refers to al-Nadr ibn al-Harith. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to him: "Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord." He said: "Rather, I follow what I found my fathers following."

(٢٢) وَمَنْ يُسْلِمْ وَجْهَهُ إِلَى اللهِ ** (22) And whoever submits his face to Allah by entrusting his affair to Him and turning towards Him with all his being, while he is a doer of good (مُحْسِنٌ) in his action (علمه), فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقى ** then he has grasped the firmest handle he has clung to the most trustworthy thing one can cling to. Al-Qummi said: [It refers to] the Wilayah (الولاية). **وَإِلَى اللهِ عاقِبَةُ الْأُمُورِ ** and to Allah belongs the outcome of all affairs since everything returns to Him.

(٢٣) وَمَنْ كَفَرَ فَلا يَحْزُنْكَ كُفْرُهُ ** (23) And whoever disbelieves, let his disbelief not grieve you. for it will not harm you. إِلَيْنا مَرْجِعُهُمْ فَنُنَبِّئُهُمْ بِما عَمِلُوا إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ. (23) To Us is their return, whereat We will inform them concerning what they did. Indeed, Allah is aware of what is in the breasts. (٢٤) نُمَتِّعُهُمْ قَلِيلاً ثُمَّ نَضْطَرُّهُمْ إِلى عَذابٍ غَلِيظٍ. (24) We let them enjoy themselves a little, then We compel them to a harsh punishment. (٢٥) وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ مَنْ خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ لَيَقُولُنَّ اللهُ ** (25) And if you ask them who created the heavens and the earth, they will surely say, 'Allah.' due to the clarity of the proof, such that they are compelled to acknowledge it (الإذعان).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Every newborn is born upon the innate disposition (الفطرة), meaning upon the knowledge (المعرفة) that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is his Creator." And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ ** (the Ayah).

And narrated from al-Jawad (peace be upon him): He was asked about the meaning of al-Wahid (الواحد - The One), and he replied: "It is the consensus of tongues upon Him in affirming His Oneness (التوحيد), just as the Mighty and Majestic said: وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ ** (the Ayah)." قُلِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ** (25) Say, 'All praise belongs to Allah!' for compelling them and forcing them to confess that which necessitates the invalidity of their belief (معتقدهم). بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** (25) But most of them do not know.** that this is binding upon them.

(٢٦) لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (26) For Allah is whatever is in the heavens and the earth. None besides Him deserves worship in them (the heavens and the earth). إِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْغَنِيُ ** (26) Indeed, Allah is the All-sufficient, independent of the praise of those who praise Him, الْحَمِيدُ ** (26) the All-laudable. the One Who deserves praise, even if He is not praised. (٢٧) وَلَوْ أَنَّ ما فِي الْأَرْضِ مِنْ شَجَرَةٍ أَقْلامٌ وَالْبَحْرُ يَمُدُّهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ سَبْعَةُ أَبْحُرٍ ** (27) Were all the trees on the earth pens, and the sea ink, with seven more seas to back it up, and the encompassing sea, with its vastness, were ink (مداداً) extended by seven seas, so the mention of the ink (المداد) was rendered unnecessary by His saying "to back it up" (بمدّه), because it is from مدّ الدّواة (supplying the inkwell) and أمدّها (supplying it), and البحر is in the accusative case (بالنصب). And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was recited: وَالْبَحْرُ مداده (and the sea is its ink). ما نَفِدَتْ كَلِماتُ اللهِ ** (27) the words of Allah would not be exhausted. writing them (the words) with those pens and that ink (المداد). إِنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ ** (27) Indeed, Allah is all-mighty, nothing frustrates Him, حَكِيمٌ (27) all-wise. no matter escapes His knowledge and wisdom. Al-Qummi: That was because the Jews asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم about the Spirit (الرّوح). So He said: الرُّوحُ مِنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّي وَما أُوتِيتُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً (The Spirit is by the command of my Lord, and you have been given of the knowledge nothing except a little). They asked: "Is this specific to us?" He said: "No, rather it is general for all people." They said: "How can this be reconciled, O Muhammad? Do you claim that you have been given nothing of knowledge except a little, while you have been given the Quran and we have been given the Torah, and you have recited: وَمَنْ يُؤْتَ الْحِكْمَةَ ** (And whoever is given wisdom) – which is the Torah – فَقَدْ أُوتِيَ خَيْراً كَثِيراً ** (has certainly been given much good)?" So Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed the verse: وَلَوْ أَنَّ ما فِي الْأَرْضِ (Were all the trees on the earth...), meaning: the knowledge of Allah is greater than that, and what you have been given, though much among you, is little in the sight of Allah.

(28) ما خَلْقُكُمْ وَلا بَعْثُكُمْ إِلَّا كَنَفْسٍ واحِدَةٍ ** (28) Your creation and your resurrection are only as a single soul. It was said:** [It is] except like the creation and resurrection of that single soul, since no affair distracts Him from another affair.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It reached us—and Allah knows best—that they said: "O Muhammad, We were created in stages: drops of sperm (نُطَفًا), then clots (عَلَقًا), then We were brought forth as another creation, as you claim. And you claim that We will be resurrected in a single hour." So Allah said: ما خَلْقُكُمْ وَلا بَعْثُكُمْ إِلَّا كَنَفْسٍ واحِدَةٍ ** (28) Your creation and your resurrection are only as a single soul. He only says to it 'Be' (كُنْ), and it is. إِنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ** (Indeed, Allah is All-hearing, All-seeing). No hearing distracts Him from another hearing, nor does any seeing distract Him from another seeing.

(٢٩) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ يُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِي النَّهارِ وَيُولِجُ النَّهارَ فِي اللَّيْلِ (29) Have you not regarded that Allah makes the night pass into the day, and makes the day pass into the night? Al-Qummi says: What decreases from the night enters into the day, and what decreases from the day enters into the night. وَسَخَّرَ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ كُلٌ ** meaning, each one of the two lights (النّيرين) يَجْرِي ** runs in its orbit (فلكه) إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى Al-Qummi says: Each one of the two runs until its limit (منتهاه); it neither falls short of it nor exceeds it. وَأَنَّ اللهَ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ (And Allah is well aware of what you do.) Knower of its essence (كنهه).

(٣٠) ** ذلِكَ ** is a reference to what was mentioned regarding the vastness of knowledge, the comprehensiveness of power, the wonders of creation, and the exclusivity of the Creator (exalted be His Name) having them. ** بِأَنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْحَقُّ وَأَنَّ ما يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ هُوَ الْباطِلُ ** (That is because Allah is the Reality, and what they invoke besides Him is the falsehood) And it was read with the yāʾ. ** وَأَنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ ** (and that Allah is the All-exalted, the All-great.) The One Who is exalted above everything and the One Who dominates it.

(٣١) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ الْفُلْكَ تَجْرِي فِي الْبَحْرِ بِنِعْمَتِ اللهِ ** (31) Do you not see that the ships sail in the sea by Allah’s blessing? By His benevolence (إحسانه) in preparing its means (أسبابه). Al-Qummi said: The ships (السفن) تَجْرِي فِي الْبَحْرِ ** (sail in the sea) by the power (قدرة) of Allah لِيُرِيَكُمْ مِنْ آياتِهِ ** (that He may show you some of His signs)—meaning His proofs (دلائله)—إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ شَكُورٍ ** (There are indeed signs in that for every patient and grateful [person]). It was said: meaning, for everyone who restrains his soul (حبس نفسه) upon contemplating the signs (آيات) of Allah, reflecting upon His bounties (آلائه), and being grateful for His favors (نعمائه). And Al-Qummi said: [It refers to] the one who is patient (يصبر) regarding poverty (الفقر) and destitution (الفاقة) and thanks Allah in all his states (أحواله). I say: Perhaps He meant by it the one who does not sail the sea seeking provision (الرزق), but takes heed (يعتبر) from the one who sails it for that purpose. And it was said that the patient and grateful person (الصبّار الشكور) is meant to be the believer (المؤمن). In the Hadith: Faith (الإيمان) is two halves: half is patience (صبر) and half is gratitude (شكر). This was narrated in al-Majma'. I say: The sailor (راكب البحر) is between fear of drowning (الغرق) and hope for salvation (الخلاص). Thus, he is constantly between affliction (بليّة) and blessing (نعمة). The affliction demands patience (الصبر) from him, and the blessing demands gratitude (الشكر) from him, so he is patient and grateful (صبّار شكور).

(٣٢) وَإِذا غَشِيَهُمْ ** (And when waves cover them) covered them (علاهم) and enveloped them (غطّاهم), meaning in the sea. مَوْجٌ كَالظُّلَلِ ** (a wave like canopies) like what shades (يُظلّ) from a mountain, or a cloud, or other things. دَعَوُا اللهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ ** (they supplicate Allah, dedicating their faith sincerely to Him) due to the removal of what conflicts with the innate disposition (الفطرة)—namely, caprice (الهوى) and imitation (التقليد)—because of the intense fear (الخوف الشديد) that afflicted them. فَلَمَّا نَجَّاهُمْ إِلَى الْبَرِّ فَمِنْهُمْ مُقْتَصِدٌ ** (But when He delivers them to the land, some of them are moderate) Al-Qummi said: meaning righteous (صالح). وَما يَجْحَدُ بِآياتِنا إِلَّا كُلُّ خَتَّارٍ ** (and none repudiates Our signs except every treacherous one) a betrayer (غدّار) who breaks the innate covenant (العهد الفطريّ) and what occurred in the sea. And al-khatr (الختر) is the most severe form of treachery (الغدر). And Al-Qummi said: al-khattār (الختّار) is the deceiver (الخدّاع). كَفُورٍ ** (ungrateful.) for the blessings (النّعم).

(33) ** يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمْ وَاخْشَوْا يَوْماً لا يَجْزِي والِدٌ عَنْ وَلَدِهِ ** (O mankind! Be wary of your Lord, and fear the day when a father will not avail his child in any way) He does not fulfill [the requirement] for him. And it was read lā yujzī (لا يجزي) from ijzāʾ (إجزاء), meaning: he does not avail/suffice. وَلا مَوْلُودٌ هُوَ جازٍ عَنْ والِدِهِ شَيْئاً إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ حَقٌ (nor will a child avail its father in any way. Indeed, Allah’s promise is true) regarding reward (الثواب) and punishment (العقاب). Al-Qummi said: That [day] is the Day of Resurrection (القيامة). فَلا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا ** (So do not let the life of the world deceive you) by its enticement. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): The world (الدنيا) is two worlds (دنياءان): a world of conveyance (دنيا بلاغ) and a cursed world (دنيا ملعونة). وَلا يَغُرَّنَّكُمْ بِاللهِ الْغَرُورُ (nor let the Arch-deceiver deceive you concerning Allah.) [The Arch-deceiver is] The Devil (الشيطان), by making you hope for repentance (التوبة) and forgiveness (المغفرة), thereby emboldening you to commit acts of disobedience (المعاصي).

(٣٤) إِنَّ اللهَ عِنْدَهُ عِلْمُ السَّاعَةِ ** knowledge of the time of its establishment وَيُنَزِّلُ الْغَيْثَ ** in its appointed measure (إنائه المقدّرة له) and the place designated for it (المحلّ المعيّن له) in His knowledge. And it was read with tashdid (بالتشديد). وَيَعْلَمُ ما فِي الْأَرْحامِ In Nahj al-Balaghah: whether male or female, ugly or beautiful, generous or stingy, wretched or happy, and who will be fuel (حطباً) for the Fire or a companion (مرافقاً) to the Prophets in the Gardens. وَما تَدْرِي نَفْسٌ ما ذا تَكْسِبُ غَداً ** of good or evil. And perhaps it resolves upon something, but then does the opposite. وَما تَدْرِي نَفْسٌ بِأَيِّ أَرْضٍ تَمُوتُ.** (34) Indeed, Allah—with Him is the knowledge of the Hour, and He sends down the rain, and He knows what is in the wombs. And no soul knows what it will earn tomorrow, and no soul knows in what land it will die.

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): These five things have not been disclosed to any close angel (ملك مقرّب) nor any sent Prophet (نبيّ مرسل), and they are among the attributes (صفات) of Allah, the Almighty.

In Nahj al-Balaghah: This is the knowledge of the Unseen (علم الغيب) which no one knows except Allah.

In al-Majma', it is narrated in the Hadith: Indeed, the keys to the Unseen (مفاتيح الغيب) are five, which no one knows except Allah, and He recited this Ayah.

It has been narrated from the Imams of Guidance (أئمّة الهدى): that these five things are not known in detail (على التّفصيل) and with certainty (والتحقيق) by anyone other than Him, the Almighty.

I say: It was only said "in detail and with certainty" because they (peace be upon them) sometimes used to inform about some of these matters in general terms (على الإجمال). And that was only through learning from the Possessor of Knowledge (تعلّماً من ذي علم), just as the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing (عَلِيمٌ), knowing all things, All-Aware (خَبِيرٌ), knowing their inner aspects (بواطنها) just as He knows their outer aspects (ظواهرها).

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surah Luqman during a night, Allah will assign angels to him during that night who will protect him from Iblis and his soldiers until he wakes up in the morning. And if he recites it during the day, they will continuously protect him from Iblis (may the curse be upon him) and his soldiers until the evening.

SURAH AL-SAJDAH

Meccan, except for three Ayahs from it, which were revealed in Medina أَفَمَنْ كانَ مُؤْمِناً ** (Is he who is a believer) up to the end of the Ayahs. The number of its Ayahs is twenty-nine according to the Basran count, and thirty according to the rest. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)
